linear_uniform.mechanism_implied: additive / QFT sum of zero-point energies

k_information.interpretation: The prior encodes K(which prior) ~ 1.58 bits of mechanism knowledge. The CC problem dissolves under log-uniform but the mechanism is unknown. The K-information residue is 'which generative process (additive vs multiplicative) set Λ?' — not fine-tuning per se.

a_technical_problem.description: QFT predicts ρ_QFT ≈ ρ_Planck; observed ρ_Λ is 10^140× smaller
a_technical_problem.status: REAL and unresolved — requires a physical mechanism

b_fine_tuning_problem.description: P(Λ ≤ Λ_max) — depends on choice of prior
b_fine_tuning_problem.conclusion: DISSOLVES under log-uniform prior; is a prior-choice artifact

c_why_this_lambda.description: Why do observers find Λ in the anthropic window?
c_why_this_lambda.status: RESOLVED by landscape + anthropic selection (all priors give N >> 1)

main_finding: The cosmological constant fine-tuning problem (b) is a prior-choice artifact. Under a log-uniform prior, P(Λ ≤ Λ_max) = 0.5587 (55.9%) — not fine-tuned. Under the classical linear prior, P = 3.84e-139 — the '10^138 orders' problem. The prior encodes ~1.6 bits of physical information about the mechanism. The residual hard problem is the technical gap (a): what mechanism makes ρ_QFT cancel to give ρ_Λ_obs? Fine-tuning (b) and the why-this-Λ question (c) are resolved or dissolved.
